color_profiles.name: Colorless / Iceland Spar
color_profiles.cause: Absence of chromophore impurities; pure CaCO3.

color_profiles.name: White / Chalk / Limestone
color_profiles.cause: Microcrystalline or fine-grained aggregate; scattering of light.

color_profiles.name: Mangano-Calcite
color_profiles.cause: Mn2+ substituting for Ca2+ in the octahedral site.

color_profiles.name: Orange / Amber Calcite
color_profiles.cause: Iron oxides and organic matter inclusions.

color_profiles.name: Blue Calcite
color_profiles.cause: Trace impurities or structural defects; exact mechanism varies.

visual_notes: Calcite's extraordinary morphological diversity makes it among the most recognizable minerals despite no single defining habit. Clear Iceland spar shows remarkable double refraction — images appear doubled. Dogtooth clusters are among the most architecturally beautiful mineral specimens.

optical_effects.fluorescence: Highly variable — many specimens fluoresce red, pink, orange, yellow, or blue under UV; mangano-calcite often fluoresces vivid pink-red under SW UV
optical_effects.iridescence: Rare iridescence from thin-film interference in some specimens
optical_effects.play_of_color: Rare iridescence from thin-film interference in some specimens
optical_effects.chatoyancy: Fibrous satin-spar calcite shows cat's eye effect
